_comment: Reinforced cauldron (Dave's Potioneering). Heavy, thick-walled construction. Highest pressure resistance of all cauldrons — survives a single brew explosion in the elastic region or just barely entering plastic. Two stages: the first is a deep structural groan with minor deformation, the second is serious deformation before breach. Very high compression multiplier — designed to handle high-pressure brews safely.

pressure._note: Thick reinforced walls: high elastic yield, only 2 stages (shows how serious this vessel takes damage), very high compression multiplier. A 800 mbar brew explosion barely touches the elastic region — this vessel is designed to survive brews. At ~1200 mbar it starts to visibly deform.
